prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_DWT_catboost', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.56) and high seasonality variance (1.02), with THETA capturing seasonality best while FT_rf dominates overall RMSE. Early horizons favor ARIMA-based models, while late horizons prefer ONLY_DWT_catboost and FT variants. The mixed tier structure with 8 tier-1 models suggests dba_combination is optimal to leverage diverse model strengths without over-relying on any single approach.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_DWT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "THETA",
  "overall_champion": "FT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ARIMA", "late": "ONLY_DWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["FT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "DWT_rf", "rf", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "FT_catboost", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["THETA", "ETS", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "DWT_svr", "CWT_catboost", "ARIMA", "svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.5565,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.56) and high seasonality variance (1.02), with THETA capturing seasonality best while FT_rf dominates overall RMSE. Early horizons favor ARIMA-based models, while late horizons prefer ONLY_DWT_catboost and FT variants. The mixed tier structure with 8 tier-1 models suggests dba_combination is optimal to leverage diverse model strengths without over-relying on any single approach."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "ridge_stacking_l250_topk5"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.35}},
  "score_preset": "robust_smape",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong per-horizon model diversity, favoring best_per_horizon and topk_mean approaches. relative_spread_mean=0.35 (≥0.25) signals high disagreement, making dba_combination (recommended by pattern_analyst_insights) essential for robust aggregation. RMSE gap between FT_rf (5096.96) and NaiveSeasonal (7179.22) is ~41%, justifying inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage=0.35. n_windows=3 requires small top_k (3) and moderate shrinkage to avoid overfitting. ridge_stacking adds stacking diversity for the heterogeneous horizon structure."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has 5 candidates with diverse types: baseline (dba_combination), selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), stacking (ridge_stacking_l250_topk5). No leakage detected - all use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection from past windows only. n_windows=3 in validation_summary confirms safe fitting window. Universe leaderboards show current candidates perform well: dba_combination leads SMAPE/MAPE, ridge_stacking variants lead RMSE, inverse_rmse_weights_k* lead POCID. No redundancy issues - each candidate serves different purpose. Score margin top2 is small (0.00095) indicating competitive set.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["ridge_stacking_l210_topk3", "trimmed_mean_r0.25"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "n_windows=3 is low, so need stronger regularization. relative_spread_mean=0.35≥0.25 indicates high disagreement favoring robust methods. Stability flag shows rmse_std_over_rmse=0.49>0.3 (unstable). Adding ridge_stacking_l210_topk3 from universe RMSE leaderboard (5397 vs current 5468) and trimmed_mean_r0.25 for stability. Current set already has good diversity - no removals needed.", "changes": ["added ridge_stacking_l210_topk3 for low n_windows regularization", "added trimmed_mean_r0.25 for stability when rmse_std_over_rmse>0.3"]}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.56) and high seasonality variance (1.02), with THETA capturing seasonality best while FT_rf dominates overall RMSE. Early horizons favor ARIMA-based models, while late horizons prefer ONLY_DWT_catboost and FT variants. The mixed tier structure with 8 tier-1 models suggests dba_combination is optimal to leverage diverse model strengths without over-relying on any single approach.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong per-horizon model diversity, favoring best_per_horizon and topk_mean approaches. relative_spread_mean=0.35 (≥0.25) signals high disagreement, making dba_combination (recommended by pattern_analyst_insights) essential for robust aggregation. RMSE gap between FT_rf (5096.96) and NaiveSeasonal (7179.22) is ~41%, justifying inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage=0.35. n_windows=3 requires small top_k (3) and moderate shrinkage to avoid overfitting. ridge_stacking adds stacking diversity for the heterogeneous horizon structure.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 5 candidates with diverse types: baseline (dba_combination), selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), stacking (ridge_stacking_l250_topk5). No leakage detected - all use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection from past windows only. n_windows=3 in validation_summary confirms safe fitting window. Universe leaderboards show current candidates perform well: dba_combination leads SMAPE/MAPE, ridge_stacking variants lead RMSE, inverse_rmse_weights_k* lead POCID. No redundancy issues - each candidate serves different purpose. Score margin top2 is small (0.00095) indicating competitive set.

statistician.rationale: n_windows=3 is low, so need stronger regularization. relative_spread_mean=0.35≥0.25 indicates high disagreement favoring robust methods. Stability flag shows rmse_std_over_rmse=0.49>0.3 (unstable). Adding ridge_stacking_l210_topk3 from universe RMSE leaderboard (5397 vs current 5468) and trimmed_mean_r0.25 for stability. Current set already has good diversity - no removals needed.
changes: added ridge_stacking_l210_topk3 for low n_windows regularization
added trimmed_mean_r0.25 for stability when rmse_std_over_rmse>0.3
